content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates solid AoA-adjacent instincts — feel-over-figure is invoked implicitly (turn 12: 'Not fix anything. Not figure out the next step'; turn 14: 'You don't have to do anything with it right now'), and there's a clear move toward feeling-the-unfelt-emotion. The coach also tracks defensiveness-as-identity-signal well (noticing the flicker, naming the pivot in turn 6, the redirection pattern in turn 8). However, signature questions and frameworks are largely absent in their verbatim or near-verbatim AoA form. The coach never asks 'what are you defending?' even when defendedness is overtly live. The 'what would happen in your body if a partner adored you openly' question — the central AoA-style move for this scenario — is gestured at by the client herself in turn 15 but never asked by the coach. No fear-triangle naming, no head-heart-gut, no 'I don't buy your story,' no explicit invitation to deconstruct the victim frame as a move. The language is warm, attuned, and competent therapy-inflected coaching with some AoA flavor, but the signature toolkit is mostly missing. Hits are appropriate where they appear; absences are notable. Few hits + all appropriate maps to 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opens with a reasonable feeling-oriented question but in generic-coaching register, not signature AoA phrasing."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Reflects the 'good enough' equation — useful read, but in standard therapy-coaching language, no AoA signature."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the flicker and the pivot — close to defensiveness-as-identity-signal read, well-timed."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'The awareness might be protecting you from feeling something' — feel-over-figure distinction implicitly invoked, appropriate to moment. Could have used 'what are you defending?' verbatim."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the unfinished sentence — appropriate non-verbal AoA move, holds the unfelt emotion edge."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Not fix anything. Not figure out the next step' — feel-over-figure invoked. 'What's it like to let it land without converting it into a plan' is AoA-adjacent."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Tender holding but slips toward soothing/inner-child language without the AoA twist; could have invited deeper somatic contact."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client herself surfaces the receiving-stance fear ('what would I do with someone who just showed up?') — coach didn't ask the body question that would have been the signature move here."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["defensiveness-as-identity-signal", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["what-are-you-defending", "if-i-were-inherently-good", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "deconstruct-the-victim-to-release-anger", "anxious-avoidant-dynamic (named only by client, not deployed by coach)", "trauma-mapping"],
  "notes": "Coach has good AoA instincts and avoids anti-AoA frames (no CBT reframing, no attachment-style diagnosis trap). But the signature question/framework layer is thin — mostly implicit feel-over-figure plus careful tracking. The body-adoration question, which is the linchpin move for this scenario, is missed."
}
